rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CARTER et al VS. PACIFIC UNION REAL ESTATE GROUP LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn In Support Of Mtn Compel Production Of Documents, Compel Deposition, And For A Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 10, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs ROBERT CARTER and BOBBIE CARTER's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, Compel Deposition, And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MURDOCH et al VS. BROCK SOLUTIONS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 10, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Denied without prejudice. Defendant Turner Construction Company has not set forth substantial evidence showing the nature and extent of its liability. (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NATHAN MARSHALL et al VS. DESOTO CAB COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 10, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT SELBY AND HUDSON CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The Court did not consider the Declaration of Miles B. Cooper or the facts stated in the Opposition that went beyond the four corners of the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC"). (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) The SAC adequately pleads facts to give rise to an alter ego theory. (SAC, 8-11.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Deft'S To Produce All Non-Privileged Documents Responsive To Pltf'S First Req. For Production Of Documents And For An Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 10, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER's Motion For Order Compelling Defendants To Produce All Non-Privileged Documents Responsive To Plaintiff's First Request For Production Of Documents And For An Award Of Sanctions. Denied. The requests are overbroad. For Request for Production no. 103, although Plaintiff is correct that, on a case by case basis, evidence of similar incidents can be probative of an employer's motive or intent regarding an individual plaintiff. (Johnson v. United Cerebral Palsy/Spastic Children's Found. of Los Angeles & Ventura Counties (2009)173 Cal.App.4th 740, 767.) However, in this case, it does not follow that Defendant must produce all evidence company-wide. Defendant's response that is has searched for any judgments from Plaintiff's geographic region for the time period from 2003 to 2011 is sufficient. Monetary sanctions of $ 2,000.00 are awarded in favor of Defendant PG&E and against Plaintiff Julie Walker to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order . The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Deft'S To Employment Form Interrogatories, Set One, And For An Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 10, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER's Motion For Order Compelling Defendants' Response to Employment Form Interrogatories, Set One, And For An Award Of Sanctions. Granted, in part. Within 10 days, Defendant PG&E shall provide further verified responses to Form Interrogatories nos. 200.4(a), 201.1(c)-(d), 201.3(d), 201.4(e)-(f), 201.6(b)-(c), 208.1(e)-(f). 208.2(d), 215.1 and 215.2. For Form Interrogatories nos. 208.2(d), 215.1, and 215.2, Defendant has not demonstrated that the responses would reveal any attorney work product. For Form Interrogatories nos. 200.4(a), 201.1(d), 201.3(d) and 201.4(f), Defendant shall provide supplemental responses that omit the qualifier "including but not limited to" so as to indicate that all responsive documents are contained within the response. For Form Interrogatories nos. 201.6(b)-(c) and 208.1(e)-(f), the discovery sought is relevant to the action. For Form Interrogatories nos. 201.1(c), 201.4(e), 208.2(d), 215.1 and 215.2, the parties have stipulated that witnesses who are employed by defendant may be subpoenaed for deposition or trial through defendant. For third party witnesses, Defendant shall identify those witnesses who were involved in the investigation of Plaintiff's claims or may have information related to them, or how defendant dealt with them. Pursuant to the agreement of the parties, Defendant shall provide the identities of witnesses who made similar complaints as Plaintiff, but Defendant's responses are limited to the geographic location where Plaintiff worked from the time period from 2003 to 2011. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
